title: UMA: Unified Motion-Action Modeling for Heterogeneous Robot Learning
summary: UMA (Unified Motion-Action Model) uses 3D object motion trajectories as a shared interface to bridge visuomotor control and dynamics modeling, treating object motion and robot actions as co-evolving variables under a masked generative objective — where the mask pattern sets both the pretraining supervision regime and the deployment inference mode. Using hindsight-relabeled motion contexts and a contrastive objective that disentangles task intent from scene geometry, it enables multi-task pretraining across heterogeneous data sources without manually annotated task instructions. Pretrained on a mixture of robot demonstrations, human videos, and simulated data, the same parameters support motion-conditioned visuomotor control, motion-based dynamics modeling, and few-shot task adaptation, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines specialized for each mode.
